title: Image-Layered World Model
description: Third-person video decomposition for reasoning and control
summary: Third-person video generation can be made more useful if it is decomposed into scene layers rather than treated as flat pixels.
asset_caption: Layer 0 background, Layer 1 objects, Layer 2 robots.
details: Target outputs: data augmentation, reward model, and inverse dynamics model.
Robot layer should support robot priors at generation time: either a manipulator URDF or a single robot-arm image as the robot-condition input.
Immediate work: survey layered video / object-centric generation methods.
risks_decisions: Layered model scope: visual decomposition only, or state-aware layers from the beginning.
Evaluation target: data augmentation quality, reward-model usefulness, or IDM recoverability.
